name: Edna Sey
bio: Singer, artist, composer, and Afro-activist. With over 10 years on stage in both national and international tours, Edna continues her solo career following the legacy of her group, the charismatic THE SEY SISTERS. Her track record includes awards for Best Musical Actress for "La tienda de los horrores" (2019) and Best Female Singer at the Enlace Funk Awards (2021). She will soon release her third album, full of Afro-Caribbean rhythms and original compositions, a multilingual Afrobeat record tinted with the Soul that characterizes her. In 2024, she created AFROKONNECTION, a safe space and networking hub for people of African descent and the diaspora, and in 2025 she launched the AFROMUSICIANS COLLECTIVE. Through her production company ESEYASAPRODUCTIONS, she unites great Afro artists in the territory to create collaborative shows.

name: Bigwé
bio: Born in Yaoundé, Cameroon, Bigwé creates from friction: African, European, queer, man. His work stems from the Afro-diasporic music that marked his childhood—pop, bikutsi, zouk, and Y2K R&B—which he fuses to shape new sounds. His music and performances embrace the fluidity of identities and their stereotypes as territories for creation and transformation, generating scenarios where complexity—desire, contradiction, performativity, spirituality, and power—is the norm.

name: Astrid Jones
bio: Actress and singer from Madrid, originally from Equatorial Guinea. As a singer, she leads her soul project Astrid Jones & The Blue Flaps, with whom she recorded her first album, "Stand Up", performing at numerous national festivals and venues, and presenting her work at the Jazz à la Cité Festival in Paris. They are currently preparing their second album, "Shine", to be released on digital platforms on February 7, 2025. Her role as Tina Turner in "Tina: The Musical" allowed her to enter the world of musical theater. In May 2024, she performed at the "Tina, One Year Later" concert with the RTVE Orchestra and Choir. She was part of the Gospel Factory choir for 15 years, working as a backing vocalist for artists like Pitingo, Marta Sánchez, Beatriz Luengo, and Raphael. As an actress, her recent works include the play "El fin" (2024) and "La Paz" at the Mérida Classical Theatre Festival. In audiovisuals, she has appeared in "La fiebre de los ricos" (2025), "Eva y Nicole" (2024), "Élite" (2023), "El regreso de la espía" (2022), and "El ministerio del tiempo" (2020).

name: Marga Mbande
bio: Singer, composer, communicator, and entertainer of Equatoguinean origin born in Barcelona, recognized for her powerful voice, dedication, and stage charisma. After standing out in 2003 as part of the R&B female duo Dlux, she started her solo career in 2012, exploring roles as a backing vocalist and MC, and touring the US Midwest. After experimenting with jazz, soul, and reggae, her latest works (Mbande Sound and Afromantik) consolidate her bet on Afro-fusion and innovation with electronic sounds. She is also the founder of the space @afrolab_basement, a support and visibility platform for other artists.

name: Bren
bio: A singer-songwriter, actress, rapper, and dancer born in Caracas, with Venezuelan, Colombian, and Spanish roots. She understands art as a borderless language, where music, body, and word blend to tell what sometimes doesn't fit into a single discipline. Her songs are born from experience, questions, contradiction, and the desire to understand the world without stopping feeling it. On stage, she deploys a hard-to-categorize energy: the strength of rap coexists with the vulnerability of showing oneself without armor; dance appears as a natural extension of music. She is currently part of Macaco's band and the Soundara Collective, while preparing the release of her new album. Her conviction is simple: art is not meant to impress, it is meant to transmit.

name: Bou
bio: A French artist, producer, life coach, and designer originally from Paris. Her journey—a unique intersection of art, entrepreneurship, and social advocacy—is a testament to her rare resilience and deep commitment to mentorship. After competing in high-level tennis, she began writing and composing music at 14. At 19, she founded her own independent label to support young artists. She performed at Barcelona's Cruïlla Festival (2023) and was featured in national press like La Vanguardia. Currently, from Paris, she organizes the monthly "Love. Faith. Resilience. (LFR)" talks and masterclasses. She is also the founder of the Black Women Entrepreneurs community in Barcelona.

name: Afrikemet
bio: "African Heroes" — A tribute to the dance orchestras of Africa from the 60s and 70s, the period of independence, when the orchestras of the great colonial hotels responded to a new society full of hope and new identities. This is when new musical styles such as Soukous, Afrobeat, High Life, and African salsa emerged, embracing the new styles from their American brothers like soul, funk, rock, and boogaloo. The band is led by artistic director and renowned Equatoguinean guitarist David Maestro.

name: DJ Owey
bio: An Afro-Spanish DJ with roots in Equatorial Guinea, based in Barcelona. Her passion for music has led her to stand out in the local scene, fusing genres such as Afrobeat, Amapiano, Dancehall, Hip Hop, and R&B to create sonic and cultural experiences. She has performed at prestigious festivals like Primavera Sound 2022, Amafest Afrobeat Festival 2023, and Rototom Sunsplash, as well as at iconic venues like Sala Apolo, Sala Razzmatazz, and Sala Jamboree.
